---
title: "Projects"
description: "A live gallery of projects published as GitHub Pages sites — pulled directly from my GitHub account so it stays current as new things go up."
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
  freeze: false   # always re-fetch from GitHub on every render
---

```{r setup}
library(httr2)
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(htmltools)
library(lubridate)
library(yaml)
library(stringr)

# Repos that have Pages enabled but aren't worth showcasing
# (templates, abandoned scaffolds, accidental forks, etc.)
exclude <- c(
  "hello-world",
  "github.io",
  "adamdennett_test.github.io",
  "adamdennett.github.io",            # academicpages template – not actively used
  "Week6Practical",
  "starter-academic",
  "BH_Secondary_Admissions_Analysis", # nothing useful published
  "BH_Reoganisation",                 # nothing useful published
  "casa_demo",                        # nothing useful published
  "ad_live_website"                   # this site itself — no recursive link
)

# Optional manual category tags by repo name — repos not listed here are "Other"
categories <- list(
  "Brighton & Hove schools"        = c("BH_Schools_2", "BH_Schools_Consultation",
                                       "bh_school_choice", "school_attainment_tool"),
  "Local government & policy"      = c("council_tax",
                                       "defibrillator-analysis"),
  "Energy & EPC"                   = c("EPC_Analysis_Website", "EPC_Data_Analysis"),
  "Teaching"                       = c("QM", "casa0007_qm", "cartosdsbootcamp"),
  "Talks & presentations"          = c("aalto_presentation_2024", "AUM2024"),
  "Maps & explorers"               = c("BrightonRestaurantsMap"),
  "Data resources & methods"       = c("Synthetic-LS-spines", "SIModelling")
)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GitHub API helpers
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

gh_token <- function() {
  Sys.getenv("GITHUB_PAT", unset = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"))
}

gh_req <- function(url) {
  req <- request(url) |>
    req_headers("Accept" = "application/vnd.github+json",
                "X-GitHub-Api-Version" = "2022-11-28") |>
    req_error(is_error = function(resp) FALSE)
  pat <- gh_token()
  if (nzchar(pat)) req <- req_auth_bearer_token(req, pat)
  req
}

fetch_repos <- function(user = "adamdennett") {
  gh_req(sprintf("https://api.github.com/users/%s/repos?per_page=100&type=owner&sort=updated", user)) |>
    req_perform() |>
    resp_body_json()
}

# Pull README.md from the repo's default branch and return its raw text
# (NULL if the repo has no README).
fetch_readme <- function(repo_full_name, default_branch) {
  url <- sprintf("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/%s/%s/README.md",
                 repo_full_name, default_branch)
  resp <- gh_req(url) |> req_perform()
  if (resp_status(resp) != 200) return(NULL)
  resp_body_string(resp)
}

# Extract YAML front-matter (if any) and remaining body from a markdown string.
# Front-matter must start at line 1 with `---` and end with `---`.
split_frontmatter <- function(text) {
  if (is.null(text) || !nzchar(text)) return(list(meta = list(), body = ""))
  # `(?s)` puts the perl regex into dot-all mode so `.` matches newlines.
  m <- regmatches(text,
                  regexec("(?s)^---[ \t]*\r?\n(.*?)\r?\n---[ \t]*\r?\n(.*)$",
                          text, perl = TRUE))[[1]]
  if (length(m) < 3) return(list(meta = list(), body = text))
  meta <- tryCatch(yaml::yaml.load(m[2]), error = function(e) list())
  if (!is.list(meta)) meta <- list()
  list(meta = meta, body = m[3])
}

# Find the first ![alt](path) image reference in a markdown body
first_image_in_body <- function(body) {
  if (!nzchar(body)) return(NULL)
  m <- regmatches(body, regexec("(?s)!\\[[^\\]]*\\]\\(([^)\\s]+)",
                                body, perl = TRUE))[[1]]
  if (length(m) >= 2) m[2] else NULL
}

# Turn an image reference into a directly-loadable URL.
# - Relative path  -> https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<repo>/<branch>/<path>
# - github.com blob URL -> rewrite to raw.githubusercontent.com (common
#   gotcha: people paste the URL of the image's page on GitHub, not the
#   raw image, and the page URL won't load as an <img src>)
# - Other absolute URL -> pass through
absolutize_image <- function(image, repo_full_name, default_branch) {
  if (is.null(image) || !nzchar(image)) return(NA_character_)
  if (grepl("^https?://github\\.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/blob/", image)) {
    return(sub("^https?://github\\.com/([^/]+)/([^/]+)/blob/",
               "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/\\1/\\2/", image))
  }
  if (grepl("^https?://", image)) return(image)
  image <- sub("^\\./", "", image)
  sprintf("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/%s/%s/%s",
          repo_full_name, default_branch, image)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pull repos and enrich with README front-matter
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

repos <- fetch_repos()

base_df <- map_dfr(repos, function(r) {
  tibble(
    name           = r$name,
    full_name      = r$full_name,
    default_branch = r$default_branch %||% "main",
    description    = r$description %||% "",
    language       = r$language %||% "",
    fork           = isTRUE(r$fork),
    archived       = isTRUE(r$archived),
    has_pages      = isTRUE(r$has_pages),
    stars          = r$stargazers_count %||% 0L,
    updated        = as_date(ymd_hms(r$updated_at)),
    repo_url       = r$html_url,
    pages_root     = sprintf("https://adamdennett.github.io/%s/", r$name)
  )
}) |>
  filter(has_pages, !archived, !(name %in% exclude)) |>
  arrange(desc(updated))

# For each project, fetch the README and parse optional front-matter.
# Returns a tibble row with optional `title`, `description`, `site`, `image`.
enrich <- function(name, full_name, default_branch) {
  readme <- tryCatch(fetch_readme(full_name, default_branch), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(readme)) {
    return(tibble(meta_title = NA_character_, meta_description = NA_character_,
                  meta_site = NA_character_, meta_image = NA_character_))
  }
  parts <- split_frontmatter(readme)
  meta <- parts$meta
  # Image fallback: first ![](...) in README body if no front-matter image
  img <- meta$image %||% first_image_in_body(parts$body)
  tibble(
    meta_title       = meta$title       %||% NA_character_,
    meta_description = meta$description %||% NA_character_,
    meta_site        = meta$site        %||% NA_character_,
    meta_image       = absolutize_image(img, full_name, default_branch)
  )
}

enriched <- pmap_dfr(
  list(base_df$name, base_df$full_name, base_df$default_branch),
  enrich
)

project_df <- bind_cols(base_df, enriched) |>
  mutate(
    display_title       = coalesce(meta_title, name),
    display_description = coalesce(na_if(meta_description, ""), na_if(description, "")),
    pages_url           = ifelse(!is.na(meta_site) & nzchar(meta_site),
                                 paste0(pages_root, sub("^/", "", meta_site)),
                                 pages_root),
    image_url           = meta_image
  )

# Attach category
cat_lookup <- stack(categories) |>
  setNames(c("name", "category")) |>
  mutate(across(everything(), as.character))
project_df <- project_df |>
  left_join(cat_lookup, by = "name") |>
  mutate(category = ifelse(is.na(category), "Other", category))

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Card renderer
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

card <- function(row) {
  has_image <- !is.na(row$image_url) && nzchar(row$image_url)
  has_desc  <- !is.na(row$display_description) && nzchar(row$display_description)

  image_block <- if (has_image) {
    a(href = row$pages_url, target = "_blank", class = "project-image-link",
      tags$img(src = row$image_url, alt = row$display_title,
               loading = "lazy", class = "project-image"))
  }

  div(class = paste("project-card", if (has_image) "with-image" else "no-image"),
      image_block,
      div(class = "project-card-body",
          h3(class = "project-title",
             a(href = row$pages_url, target = "_blank", row$display_title)),
          if (has_desc)
            p(class = "project-desc", row$display_description)
          else
            p(class = "project-desc project-desc-empty", em("(no description)")),
          div(class = "project-meta",
              if (nzchar(row$language))
                span(class = "badge lang", row$language),
              span(class = "badge date",
                   paste0("Updated ", format(row$updated, "%b %Y"))),
              if (row$stars > 0)
                span(class = "badge stars", paste0("★ ", row$stars)),
              if (row$fork)
                span(class = "badge fork", "fork")
          ),
          div(class = "project-links",
              a(href = row$pages_url, target = "_blank", "Live site →"),
              a(href = row$repo_url,  target = "_blank", "Source")
          )
      )
  )
}

render_section <- function(df, heading) {
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
  tagList(
    h2(heading),
    div(class = "project-grid",
        lapply(split(df, seq_len(nrow(df))), card)
    )
  )
}
```

```{r summary}
n_with_image <- sum(!is.na(project_df$image_url) & nzchar(project_df$image_url))
htmltools::p(
  class = "project-summary",
  sprintf("%d live project sites across %d categories — sorted within each category by most recent activity. %d have a featured image.",
          nrow(project_df),
          length(unique(project_df$category)),
          n_with_image)
)
```

```{r render-categories}
#| output: asis
ordered_cats <- c(names(categories), "Other")
ordered_cats <- ordered_cats[ordered_cats %in% unique(project_df$category)]

sections <- lapply(ordered_cats, function(cat) {
  sub <- project_df |> filter(category == cat)
  render_section(sub, cat)
})

cat("\n```{=html}\n")
cat(as.character(htmltools::tagList(sections)))
cat("\n```\n")
```

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## How this page works (and how to customise per-project)

This page is built from a live call to the GitHub API at render time — it lists
every repository under [adamdennett](https://github.com/adamdennett) that has
GitHub Pages enabled, minus a small manual exclude list of templates and
scaffolds. Categories are assigned by name in the setup chunk of
`projects.qmd`; anything uncategorised falls into "Other".

To override what shows up for a given repo, add a YAML front-matter block at
the top of that repo's `README.md`:

```yaml
---
title: "A more readable project title"
description: "One-line description that will replace the GitHub repo description."
site: brighton_analysis.html  # path under the Pages root (e.g. for repos without an index.html)
image: featured.png           # relative path in the repo, or a full URL
---
```

All four keys are optional. If `image` is omitted, the first `![](...)` image
reference in the README body is used as a fallback. Rebuild the site to refresh.
:::
